overview.about: The Great Barrier Reef off Queensland is the world's largest coral reef system — 2,300km of over 2,900 individual reefs and 900 islands. It is the only living organism visible from space. Home to 1,500 fish species, 4,000 mollusc species and 30 species of whales and dolphins, it is Earth's richest marine ecosystem.

highlights.label: World's Largest Coral Reef System
highlights.desc: 2,300km of 2,900+ individual reefs — the only living structure visible from space, teeming with 1,500 fish species

highlights.label: Humpback Whale Migration
highlights.desc: June–November brings over 2,000 humpback whales through reef waters — snorkelling with them is life-defining

highlights.label: Whitsunday Islands Access
highlights.desc: 74 pristine tropical islands including Whitehaven Beach — world-ranked with silica sand so fine it's cool to the touch

highlights.label: UNESCO & Biodiversity Emergency
highlights.desc: Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site under threat from climate change — visiting responsibly funds vital conservation

overview.about: Great Barrier Reef in Queensland, AU showcases the breathtaking natural wealth of Australia's landscapes and marine environments. One of the Southern Hemisphere's most spectacular natural wonder destinations, it draws travellers with its raw beauty, unique wildlife and the sense of standing at the very edge of the world.

highlights.label: Biodiversity Hotspot
highlights.desc: Home to rare and endemic species — flora and fauna found nowhere else on the planet within pristine ecosystems

highlights.label: World-Renowned Trekking
highlights.desc: Trails from leisurely nature walks to multi-day high-altitude expeditions with breathtaking panoramic rewards

highlights.label: Wildlife Encounters
highlights.desc: Spot iconic wildlife in their natural habitat — guided walks dramatically increase sighting rates of rare species

highlights.label: Dramatic Natural Landscapes
highlights.desc: Awe-inspiring vistas of mountains, gorges, waterfalls and geological formations sculpted over millions of years
